title: Area of Trapezium, Rhombus and Polygons
subject: Mathematics
learningOutcomes: Calculate the area of a trapezium using parallel bases $a, b$ and height $h$.
Understand rhombus area via diagonal lengths $d_1, d_2$.
Interactively adjust parametric sliders to observe real-time area scaling.

objectives.text: Calculate the area of a trapezium with $a=10, b=6, h=5$.
Adjust the height slider to observe step-by-step KaTeX updates.

theory: Area of Trapezium $A = \frac{1}{2}(a+b)h$. Area of Rhombus $A = \frac{1}{2}d_1 d_2$. Area of General Polygon is calculated by splitting into triangles and trapeziums.
hints: Move the Parallel Side sliders $a$ and $b$ to change base geometry.
Switch shapes using the 2D Figures toolbar tab.

tasks.instruction: Increase the Parallel Side A slider above 8.

observationPrompts: What happens to the area of a trapezium if both parallel sides are doubled?

realWorldExamples.title: Roof & Bridge Trusses
realWorldExamples.description: Civil engineers calculate the surface area of trapezoidal trusses for material estimation.

commonMistakes: Confusing height (perpendicular distance) with non-parallel side lengths.
